nist_bss_143_page.title: NIST publication page for NBS BSS 143

nist_bss_143_pdf.title: Investigation of the Kansas City Hyatt Regency Walkways Collapse, NBS Building Science Series 143

nist_incident_page.title: NIST Walkway Collapse Kansas City Missouri 1981

public_release_blockers: YouTube Content ID and music claim status must still be checked after private upload processing.
rights_and_usage_notes.source_quote_policy: Use brief citation/reference notes only; do not reproduce long passages from source pages or the report in public metadata.
rights_and_usage_notes.source_use: References are used for fact-checking and public source notes. The final video uses generated source art and original narration/music assets, not archival NIST/ASCE images.

asce_article.title: The Hyatt Regency Walkway Collapse, ASCE Civil Engineering Source

basis: NIST/NBS BSS 143 abstract and summary lines from extracted text around 248-256 and 339-348.
verified_claims.claim: The Hyatt atrium had suspended walkways at the second, third, and fourth floor levels; the second-floor walkway was directly below and suspended from the fourth-floor walkway, while the third-floor walkway was offset and independently suspended.

basis: NIST/NBS BSS 143 extracted text around 256-259 and 1067-1068.
verified_claims.claim: The second- and fourth-floor walkways fell to the atrium floor, with the fourth-floor walkway coming to rest on top of the lower walkway.
NIST identified insufficient load capacity in the box beam-hanger rod connection at the fourth-floor walkway as the probable failure mechanism.

basis: NIST/NBS BSS 143 extracted text around 268-278 and 439; ASCE article saved at paragraph lines around 1422.
verified_claims.claim: Changing from continuous hanger rods to two sets of rods doubled the load transferred by the fourth-floor box beam-hanger rod connections.

basis: ASCE saved page line around 1421; NIST/NBS BSS 143 chronology line around 2334 for time of collapse.
verified_claims.claim: The collapse occurred during a public event in the atrium; public-facing casualty context may state 114 killed and 216 injured when needed, but marketing copy should avoid sensational framing.

youtube_metadata.description:
Hyatt Regency is a Cascade Effects episode about how small signals become system consequences.

A small connection change turned a public atrium into a hidden load-path failure.

Runtime: about 14 minutes.

Cascade Effects traces failures through mechanism, incentives, and institutional blind spots.
youtube_metadata.title: Hyatt Regency
